title: द्वितीयोनुवाकः
pada: नमो॑ । भ॒वस्य॑ । हे॒त्यै । जग॑ताम् । पत॑ये । नमो॒ ॥
krama: नमो॑ भ॒वस्य॑ । भ॒वस्य॑ हे॒त्यै । हे॒त्यै जग॑ताम् । जग॑ता॒-म्पत॑ये । पत॑ये॒ नमो॒ । नमो॒ इति॑ नमो॒ ॥

sayana.text:
'Bhavah' is worldly existence (Samsara); 'heti' is its weapon. Salutation be to Rudra who cuts (destroys) Samsara. Salutation be to Rudra who is the protector of the worlds. (6)

Bhatta-Bhaskara-Bhashya: 'Namo bhavasya hetyai'. 'Bhavah' is Samsara; 'heti' is the weapon; to the cutter of Samsara. Or: to the form of that injury which is the cause of Samsara, salutation to him in that form. [Grammatical note on the word 'heti']. 'Jagatam pataye namah'—to the Lord of the worlds like the Earth, etc. [Grammatical note on the word 'jagatam'].
...

abhinava_shankara.text:
80                    Śrī Rudra Bhāṣyam

---

    "Salutation to the weapon (heti) of Bhava (Śiva the cause of existence); salutation to the Lord of the worlds (jagatāṃ pataye namaḥ)."

Thus through five mantras, the Lord has been saluted with demonstrations of his lordship over certain specific things such as the directions and so forth. Now, since it is impossible to salute him individually in respect of his sovereignty over each and every thing, the sixth mantra is employed to salute him as the "Lord of the worlds" (jagatpati): "Namo bhavasya iti" — Bhava means saṃsāra (the cycle of existence); the heti (weapon) which cuts it — meaning he is the one who severs the stream of worldly existence (saṃsāravṛtta). He is the lord (pati) of all the worlds (jagatāṃ), both moving and unmoving. To him may there be salutation. Although the lordship over the entire world, from Brahmā to the stationary, was already stated by [the mantra] "Paśūnāṃ pataye namaḥ" — [the difference is that] there, it was stated in the sense of his being the cause of the bondage of saṃsāra; here, the distinction is that he is the liberator from saṃsāra. Both aspects, however, are attributes of Brahman.

    "Salutation to Rudra (the one who dispels affliction), to the one who stretches the bow (ātatāvitane), to the lord of the fields (kṣetrāṇāṃ pataye namaḥ)."

Now, to further support what has already been said about his lordship over the world, and in order to salute the Blessed Lord again through [the demonstration of] his sovereignty over various particular things — in the manner of a persistent supplicant walking again and again — the commentary begins the seventh [mantra] and those following: "Namo Rudrāya iti" — Rudra is he who dispels (drāvaka) the suffering of saṃsāra; hence, because he protects (avati, i.e., rakṣati) with the stretched (ātatena) bow, he is Ātatāvitin. Kṣetrāṇi means bodies (śarīrāṇi), as the Smṛti says: "This body, O son of Kuntī, is called the kṣetra (the field)" — he who is the lord of these bodies is kṣetrāṇāṃ patiḥ, to him may there be salutation. Just as in ordinary life the inhabitants of a land are protected by the overlord of that land —
abhinava_shankara.sanskrit:
८०                    श्रीरुद्रभाष्यम् ।

    नमो भवस्य हेत्यै जगतां पतये नम: ॥

   इत्थं पञ्चभिर्मन्त्रैदिंगादिकतिपयवस्त्वाधिपत्यप्रदर्शनपूर्व-
कमीश्वरो नमस्कृत:; इदानीं प्रतिवस्त्वाघिपत्यवचनपूर्वक-
नमस्कारस्याशक्यतया जगत्पतित्वेन तं नमस्कर्तुं षष्ठो मन्त्र:
भयुक:— नमो भवस्येति । भव: संसार:, तस्य च्छेद्ने
हेति: आयुघम्, संसारवृत्तस्य च्छेत्तेत्यर्थ: । स्थावरजङ्गमानां
लोकानां स्वामी जगतां पति:, तस्मै नम: । यद्यपि
'पशूनां पतये नम:' इत्यनैव ब्रह्मादिस्थावरान्तसकलजग-
त्पतित्वमुक्तकम्, तथापि संसारपाशबन्धहेतुत्वेन तदुक्तकम्;
अत्र संसारमोचकत्वेनेति भेद:, उभयोरपि ब्रह्मघर्मत्वात् ॥

    नमो रुद्रायाततावितने क्षेत्राणां पतये
नम: ॥

   अथ उक्तमेव जगत्पतित्वसुपलपादयितुं श्रमाबधि घाव-
न्न्यायेन पुनरपि तत्तद्वस्त्वाघिपत्येन भगवन्तं नमस्कर्तुमारभे
सप्तमादिभि:— नमो रुद्रायेति । रुद्र: संसारदु:खद्रावक:,
अत एव आत्वज्येन धनुषा अवति रक्षतीति आततावितने;
क्षेत्राणि शरीराणि, 'इदं शरीरं कौन्तेय क्षेत्रमित्यभिधीयते'
इति स्मृते:, तेषां स्वामी क्षेत्राणां पति:, तस्मै नम: इत्य-
र्थ: । लोके हि देशाधिपतिकलत्रपद्रेभ्यो देशवासिनो रक्षित,
